$schema: SSH-driven equivalent of Test-Start.guest.ubuntu.desktop.json. Selected automatically when test-config.json has keystrokeMechanism='SSH'. Hybrid: OCR for the two pre-install dismissals (GRUB menu, autoinstall confirmation) — sshd doesn't exist yet — then SSH for everything post-install. SSH key auth bypasses the `passwd --expire ubuntu` aging that breaks console password login, so the OCR password-change dance is skipped entirely.
$actions: See possible actions in the actions.json file
description: Ubuntu Desktop (SSH): OCR-dismiss GRUB + Install confirmation, then wait for cloud-init's first-boot openssh-server and run the update script over SSH.

steps.description: Dismiss GRUB menu — pre-install, sshd doesn't exist yet, OCR is the only option

label: Install Ubuntu
Install
lnstall
steps.description: Click Install via OCR-located coordinates. Replaces the old tabCount approach which drifted across installer UI revisions. For guest.ubuntu.server this step is not needed — subiquity's interactive-sections:[] runs fully unattended.

Wait for OS install + reboot + cloud-init's first-boot openssh-server install. Replaces the OCR steps for 'Not listed?' and the password-change dance — those are fragile against Ubuntu 24.04+ GDM UI changes, and SSH key auth makes the password-aging dance moot.
Smoke test: confirm session identity + that the autoinstall hostname stuck
Equivalent of the OCR-driven fetchAndExecute in the keystroke variant
Reboot — sudo issues the call and SSH connection drops as expected (allowFailure)
